items.title: Tasmania logs its hottest May day in recorded history

children.content: "We've broken records from Strahan to Launceston, down through Hobart," says the Bureau of Meteorology.

gallery.altText: A fountain in a public park. It's a hot, sunny day and there are a couple of children playing in the fountain.

thumbnail.altText: A fountain in a public park. It's a hot, sunny day and there are a couple of children playing in the fountain.

items.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

items.title: CCTV offline during knife incident at youth detention centre

children.content: Security cameras at Ashley Youth Detention Centre were offline when a detainee threatened a staff member with a knife during a cooking workshop, documents reveal.

image.altText: Exterior photos of a youth detention centre.

items.title: Who's who in Tasmania's upper house elections this Saturday

children.content: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

gallery.altText: Six cards with candidate faces on them.

thumbnail.altText: Six cards with candidate faces on them.

items.title: Mother haunted by letters after two-year-old son killed in murder-suicide

children.content: A coronial inquest hears letters that arrived three days after an apparent murder-suicide in Lismore outlined a father's deadly plans.

image.altText: A smiling, blonde little boy sitting on a chair holding a cup.

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

children.content: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

gallery.altText: A man and woman cradling two newborns, with another two in cribs behind them
A newborn baby in a floral outfit
A nurse hands a newborn baby to a woman

items.title: Beaches evacuated as sharks swarm towards 25-tonne whale carcass

children.content: The sperm whale carcass was towed from Era Beach in the Royal National Park to Bellambi Boat Ramp in Wollongong this afternoon.

image.altText: image of boat ramp with boat to left, dead whale in centre and a digger hoisting it up.

items.title: Actor on Rebel Wilson film accused of breaching NDA, court hears

children.content: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

image.altText: Charlotte MacInnes arrives at Federal Court of Australia

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

gallery.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

thumbnail.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
A wide angle shot of an older white man sitting alone in a large shed.
A trio of young Aboriginal boys climb out of a public pool.

items.title: Bulldogs off to flyer against Freo as Crows and Power meet in Showdown

children.content: The injury-hit Western Bulldogs welcome a high-flying Fremantle side to Docklands as Adelaide and Port Adelaide meet in the Showdown. Follow live.

image.altText: Laitham Vandermeer lays a heavy tackle on Andrew Brayshaw

items.title: Storm out to avoid historic loss, Cowboys stun Bulldogs in Sydney

children.content: Jason Taumalolo joins legend Johnathan Thurston in playing a record 294th match for the Cowboys before the Melbourne Storm look to avoid a seventh consecutive defeat against the Dolphins. Follow live.

image.altText: Craig Bellamy head coach of the Melbourne Storm speaking during a press conference

items.title: Lessons should have been learnt prior to NDIS rollout, integrity boss says

children.content: About $3.7 billion of National Disability Insurance Scheme money was handed out inappropriately last financial year, the scheme's integrity chief has told a parliamentary hearing.

image.altText: A close up of an NDIS sign next to a building

items.title: Bondi royal commission interim report careful but ultimately damning

children.content: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

image.altText: Police officers at Bondi Beach seven days after terrorist attack

avatar.alt: A man with closely-cropped dark hair, a moustache and beard poses for a headshot in a studio, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Is the new Foo Fighters the best album of April?

children.content: Come for Dave Grohl and co's latest. Stay for club-facing collabs, lush disco, homegrown releases and a pair of Québécois aliens making left-field instrumental jams.

children.content: It's time to sit back after a big week in news and test how much you remember.

items.title: Australian screen industry as 'intergenerational' as housing

children.content: Now in its 40th year, the annual Screen Forever conference attracts hundreds of industry leaders to look at the past, present and future of Australian screen content.

image.altText: A man stands behind a podium and in front of a projector

items.title: The battery rebate became a victim of its success. Here's what is changing

children.content: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

gallery.altText: Leanne Phillips stands next to a work truck in a driveway in Toowoomba, April 2026.

items.title: Melbourne teacher accused of installing bathroom camera denied bail

children.content: Parents of children attending Kingswood Primary School filled the Moorabbin Magistrates Court on Friday, intending to see accused teacher Timothy Blamires.

items.title: May temperature records fall across Victoria as warm weather continues

children.content: Weather records fall across the state, as a spell of summer-like conditions tips into the first day of May.

image.altText: Bollards by the waterfront in Geelong

items.title: Former teacher jailed for grooming teenage boy

children.content: A woman who groomed and sexually abused a teenage boy while she was working as a high school teacher near Newcastle has been sentenced to at least two years in prison.

image.altText: Women standing outside glass doors.

items.title: Family of Tiwi Islands sistergirl calls for life sentence for man who killed her

children.content: The family of a beloved Tiwi Islands sistergirl has pleaded for the man who killed her to be handed a life sentence, describing more than a year of sleepless nights, heartbreak and grief.

image.altText: A family stands in front of the NT Supreme Court looking sad and wearing Justice for Lena shirts.

items.title: Banksy behind overnight reveal of new statue in central London

children.content: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.

gallery.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

thumbnail.altText: A statue of a man carrying a flag, blinded by it, about to walk off his statue plinth.

items.title: Community 'disappointed' by Sheraton high-rise approval

children.content: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.

image.altText: An artist impression of a luxury 21 storey resort

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

image.altText: Two women and a man stand near a fence with a bouquet of flowers attached to it, with trees behind

children.content: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

image.altText: am illustration of a polio virus particle against a black background

items.title: LIV Golf is falling apart on a flawed premise and a broken promise

children.content: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

items.title: How a future Bronco hero Jesse Southwell broke Queensland's heart

children.content: In her final act as a Novocastrian before heading to Brisbane, Jesse Southwell kicked the first field goal of her life to deliver victory for New South Wales in Origin I.

items.title: Melbourne and Sydney record large house price falls as growth slows across the country

children.content: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

image.altText: A new housing estate stretches as far as the eye can see.

items.title: Tough-on-crime NT government snubs youth incarceration Senate inquiry

children.content: The inquiry heard calls for more scrutiny over how Commonwealth money is spent in the NT, which is trending backwards on seven Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: A group of young Indigenous children are playing basketball inside the Don Dale Youth Detention Centre.

items.title: Locals fear for jobs as Port Pirie smelter's future in doubt

children.content: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

image.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

image.altText: Matt Glaetzer smiles and clenches his fist

items.title: Review finds fire ant eradication can succeed if 'mistrust and misinformation' are fixed

children.content: An independent review finds fire ant eradication is still achievable, but major issues, including poor communication, public mistrust and strategy confusion, must be fixed first.

gallery.altText: A pile of bright coloured ants pile together to create a raft on water. the photo is so close you can see a reflection.

thumbnail.altText: A pile of bright coloured ants pile together to create a raft on water. the photo is so close you can see a reflection.
